## [00:00 - 03:02]

So what I actually want to do with you guys is create a smart list. A smart list is a list that exists within your CRM platform or your customer relationship platform. And what it does is it segments your list based on whatever criteria you decide to set up. So think if you could create a list of just people that liked puppies, and it would literally segment your list down to just people that liked puppies, because maybe on like International Dog Day you want to send an email out to everybody that likes puppies, you could do that. And it's called a smart list because it's smart in how it puts together. What you're going to do to create your smart list is if you think about it, smart lists are based off of your contacts, right? So what we're going to do is we're going to come over to the left-hand side here, and we're going to find contacts right in the middle. Everybody see my mouse where I'm looking at with the contact here? Okay. We're going to click on contacts, and it defaults to all. Okay, so where you guys see all up here, this is everybody that's in this demo setup. You guys will eventually have much larger lists than this. This is just the demo setup that we have. And what we actually want to do is create one of those lists. So if we start by just saying, well, let's create a list of anybody that hasn't been contacted in the last 90 days. That sound like a good idea? Okay. So what we're going to do is we're going to say filters. We're just going to click on add a filter. And now there's already filters in here. So these are the most used ones. And then these are ones based on contact information. And there's some other categories in here. Okay. Opportunity info, et cetera. But what we're going to do is we're going to search for filters, and we can either create our own filter, which I'll show you guys how to do as well. But let's say that we wanted to create one that was just based off of referrals or contact last activity. Okay. So activity is what you're looking for because there's different activity types. I can say is empty, meaning they're in the system and no activity has ever occurred with this customer, which in that case, I don't know how they would have gotten in the system, is not empty, meaning there was some activity with them at some point, more than less than or a range. So what we're going to say is more than, right? And then we just put 90 days. Okay. So I want to know if the last activity related to this contact was more than 90 days ago. I could also do a dropdown here and I could say minutes, hours, days, months, years, right?
